title: Local-warp registration check (shimmer suspect)
oneline: Scorer works; found 4 genuinely warped shipped pages; big-nudge hypothesis refuted
summary: Built a tile-wise cross-correlation displacement scorer (native res, gain-based confidence) and ran it over all 188 committed raws offline in ~3 min. The big-global-nudge pages (pig-wide, excavator-wide, stegosaurus-wide) are among the cleanest — the nudge/warp correlation hypothesis is refuted. But the scorer surfaced real local warp the gates missed: shapes/star-tall's night sparkle is drawn ~12px off its chalk and renders as a visible double sparkle in the simulated composite; pterodactyl-tall (night), umbrella-wide and duck-wide (light) also carry genuine 4-10px feature drift. Proposed gate: fail confident local warp >= 4px (gain 1.3-10), warn >= 3px.
tried: Per-tile edge-map cross-correlation (128px tiles, +/-12px search, smallest-magnitude tie-break) between source line art (chalk for night, pen for light) and each committed raw fill
Gain (best/zero-offset score) and peak (best/mean) as per-tile confidence signals
Full-catalog scan: 94 night + 94 light raws, ranked by confident local warp
Hypothesis test: 3 known big-nudge pages vs the 91-page population
Reconstructing historical nudges from the extendWith:'copy' edge-smear band
Heatmap/quiver overlays, red-vs-cyan worst-tile crops, simulated night-composite reveal crops (lib/night-composite.mjs)
worked: The scorer: ~1s/page offline, clean separation (87/94 night and 82/94 light raws under 2px)
Gain filter separates plateau noise (~1.0), genuine displacement (1.3-10), and missing/whitened features (>10)
Found a shipped visible defect: star-tall's night sparkle doubled in the final composite
Hypothesis answered decisively: big global nudges do NOT correlate with local warp (big-nudge pages rank 27th-89th of 94, all warpMax <= 1px)
failed: Historical nudge reconstruction from the edge-replication band (lossy webp + flat margins erase it), so the correlation test used only the 3 known nudge values vs population
Live run-splotch reveal-edge inspection (impractical in sandbox; simulated composite used instead)
limitations: Aperture problem: straight-line-only tiles slide along the line direction (shapes/triangle-wide's 13.4px is inflated; true fringe ~1-2px) — needs an orientation-dispersion guard before gating
Chalk-whitened solids leak through as false positives (space/astronaut-tall); whitening pen solids out of the reference would fix it
Dark re-inked night pages (vehicles/train-wide) give noisy fields

comparisons.label: star-tall night worst tile: source vs fill lines, then what ships
comparisons.note: red=chalk sparkle, cyan=fill's sparkle ~12px up-left; the composite shows both — a double sparkle in shipped dark mode

comparisons.label: genuine warp (umbrella flower) vs clean big-nudge control (pig-wide)
comparisons.note: umbrella's corner flower self-drifted ~5-7px (the ant-wide failure mode, shipped); pig-wide (-11 global nudge) registers perfectly

images.caption: star-tall night: fill's sparkle (cyan) drawn ~12px from the chalk's (red)
Simulated final dark-mode composite: visible double sparkle at the same spot
pterodactyl-tall night: background star filled offset/shrunken inside its chalk outline
umbrella-wide light: corner flower self-drifted ~5-7px (14 tiles >=3px)
duck-wide light: systematic ~3-4px fringing across the wing (18 tiles >=3px)
False positive: aperture problem on a straight diagonal — true fringe only 1-2px
False positive: chalk-whitened solid — fill has no edges there (gain 82)
Astronaut composite proves that tile renders correctly
Control: pig-wide (global nudge -11) registers cleanly; its 8px tile is plateau noise (gain 1.02)
train-wide night displacement heatmap: warp concentrated in the re-inked track rows
